text.content:
✦ Celerity

Level 1: Cat's Grace

Cost: Free | Pool: -- | Type: Utility

Passive. You always land on your feet. All balance-related rolls gain a bonus equal to your Celerity rating. You can walk across tightropes, narrow ledges, and slippery surfaces without rolling. Falls from any height deal half damage (round down). Your movements are unnaturally fluid — mortals may notice something 'off' about you on a Wits + Awareness roll (difficulty 4).

Level 1: Rapid Reflexes

Cost: Free | Pool: -- | Type: Combat

Passive. You can always act first in a combat turn without rolling initiative — you simply go first among those without this power. If multiple vampires have Rapid Reflexes, they compare Wits + Celerity to determine order among themselves. You can also use your free action to dodge ranged attacks, including firearms, which is normally impossible.

Level 2: Fleetness

Cost: 1 Rouse | Pool: -- | Type: Combat

Add your Celerity rating to your Dexterity for non-combat physical actions (running, dodging, acrobatics) and to your Defense rating in combat. Your movement speed doubles. Lasts one scene. While active, you move with visible supernatural speed — running appears as a blur to mortal observers. This is a Masquerade risk if used openly.

Level 3: Blink

Cost: 1 Rouse | Pool: Dex + Athletics (if precision matters) | Type: Combat

Move up to 50 meters in the blink of an eye — literally faster than human perception. You seem to disappear and reappear. Can be used to close distance to an enemy, escape a grapple, or cross a room before anyone can react. Cannot be used to dodge an attack already in progress. Walls and obstacles still block you. Can be used once per turn.

Level 3: Traversal

Cost: 1 Rouse | Pool: Dex + Athletics | Type: Combat

Run along vertical surfaces (walls, building faces) and across liquids (water, pools) as though they were level ground. You must keep moving — stopping causes you to fall or sink normally. You can run up a building face, across a river, or along a ceiling for a number of turns equal to your Celerity rating. Combine with Blink for spectacular movement.

Level 4: Draught of Elegance

Cost: 1 Rouse | Pool: -- | Type: Utility

Feed a mortal or ghoul your blood to grant them Celerity 1 (Cat's Grace or Rapid Reflexes, your choice) for one night. The recipient must drink directly from you (1 Rouse worth). Only one recipient at a time. The recipient does not gain any other vampiric traits. Useful for empowering ghouls or mortal allies before a dangerous situation.

Level 4: Unerring Aim

Cost: 1 Rouse | Pool: -- | Type: Combat

Take one turn to aim. On your next turn, your single ranged attack (firearms, thrown weapons) automatically hits — no roll required. The target cannot dodge (even with Celerity). The shot deals normal damage plus bonus damage equal to your Celerity rating. This power can only be used once per scene. Does not work on targets behind full cover.

Level 5: Lightning Strike

Cost: 1 Rouse | Pool: -- | Type: Combat

Your next melee attack is so fast that the target cannot defend against it — their Defense is reduced to 0. You add your Celerity rating as bonus damage. The attack is invisible to the naked eye; mortals see only the aftermath. Can be used once per turn. Combine with weapons for devastating effect. Cannot be used in the same turn as Blink.

Level 5: Split Second

Cost: 2 Rouse | Pool: -- | Type: Combat

Take two full actions in a single combat turn. This includes two attacks, an attack and a power activation, or any other combination. Each action is resolved separately with its own dice pool. You may even split movement between actions. However, both actions suffer a 2-dice penalty. This power can be used every turn but each use costs a Rouse check. If combined with Fleetness, the dice penalty is reduced to 1.
